summary: Re-verified the Reown AppKit (WalletConnect) frontend wallet-login UI end-to-end on the live preview environment. All 5 requested checks pass: (1) /login Connect Wallet button (data-testid='wallet-connect-btn') opens the <w3m-modal> AppKit modal listing multiple wallets (WalletConnect/QR CODE row, Binance Wallet, MetaMask, SafePal, Trust Wallet, Search Wallet 530+). (2) Mobile path: clicking the WalletConnect row swaps the modal into the QR view — DOM shows 'WalletConnect | Scan this QR Code with your phone | Copy link' and a visible QR canvas with the W3M logo center watermark (confirmed via screenshot). (3) /signup Connect Wallet button is present and opens the same AppKit modal. (4) Zero uncaught JS errors and zero non-benign console errors while opening the modal and navigating to the QR view (telemetry/config endpoints to pulse.walletconnect.org, cca-lite.coinbase.com, api.web3modal.org/config, config.reown.com are blocked as expected and AppKit falls back to local defaults — already noted in iteration_4 as a known-benign preview-env infra warning). (5) REGRESSION: email/password login with hajraanwar157@gmail.com / NewCavi@2025 successfully redirects to /app dashboard. Per task scope, real wallet signing on a phone or extension was NOT attempted (cannot be performed headlessly); backend signature verification was already proven via the EVM/Solana keypair-signing script in the previous iteration.
critical_code_review_comments: WalletButtons.js: pendingRef is used to gate runLogin() after AppKit reports isConnected — works, but if the user closes the modal without selecting a wallet, pendingRef stays true. Next time they reconnect (or if AppKit silently restores a previous session on mount), runLogin() may auto-fire. Consider also resetting pendingRef inside an AppKit 'modal close' subscription or on a useEffect cleanup.

appkit.js: createAppKit() is invoked at module import time with no error boundary. The remote-config fetch failure is logged as a warning by AppKit itself (benign in this preview env), but if projectId is ever missing/invalid in production the modal won't render and the user just sees the button do nothing. Suggest reading projectId and surfacing a visible 'wallet login temporarily unavailable' fallback when REACT_APP_REOWN_PROJECT_ID is empty.
WalletButtons.js readableError() handles common rejection codes well but doesn't surface chain-mismatch errors (e.g., user connects on an unsupported chain). Minor UX gap.

Solana base58 signature is encoded inline via base58encode(new Uint8Array(sig)) — fine, but res?.signature ?? res can silently pass an unexpected return shape from a non-standard Solana wallet. A typeof check + explicit 'unsupported wallet response' error would be safer.
context_for_next_testing_agent: To verify the Reown AppKit WalletConnect modal in a headless run: (a) click [data-testid='wallet-connect-btn'] on /login or /signup, wait ~3-4s for <w3m-modal> to render, (b) the modal is a custom element with extensive shadow-DOM nesting — clicking specific wallet rows by tag (wui-list-wallet) does NOT work because internal tags vary by AppKit version; instead use Playwright's text engine which pierces shadow DOM: `page.locator('text=WalletConnect').first.click(force=True)`. This reliably opens the QR view ('Scan this QR Code with your phone' + 'Copy link' control). (c) To inspect shadow-DOM text contents, walk recursively into el.shadowRoot in page.evaluate. (d) Benign console warnings to ignore: 'Failed to fetch remote project configuration', net::ERR_* on pulse.walletconnect.org / cca-lite.coinbase.com / api.web3modal.org/config / config.reown.com — these are blocked by the preview egress and AppKit falls back to local defaults.
rca of the issue: No bug found. All requested wallet-connect UI verification checks pass.
